element_introduction_order.element: Basic movement (speed control, limited horizontal repositioning)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Forward momentum cannot be stopped; player learns pacing control
Temporary invincibility, laser missiles, score multipliers
Resource scarcity; must progress before counter reaches zero
Unrestricted 360° movement enabled; pattern recognition required
Optional collection for scoring; tracked on status bar
Increasing surrealistic landscape complexity; synergy with movement skills

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating quadrant of enemies without time expiration
typical_tension_triggers: Approaching end of time counter in quadrant
Large canyon gaps requiring precision jump
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Constant forward momentum with cyclic quadrant-based tension peaks at timer expirations and boss encounters; relief granted through power-up discovery and safe quadrant completion

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Pillar-mounted power-ups encourage lateral exploration within narrow forward-scrolling corridor; color palette and bright visuals highlight interactive elements

difficulty_curve.early_game: World 1 introduces blob monsters, canyons, and time pressure in controlled manner; player learns movement pacing and basic jumping
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Worlds 2-4 increase enemy variety and obstacle complexity; surrealistic landscape becomes denser; quadrant time pressure becomes tighter
difficulty_curve.late_game: Worlds 5-8 combine multiple enemy types, complex terrain, and increased Serpentbeast AI difficulty; cumulative skill demands peak
notable_spikes: First Serpentbeast encounter (World 1 finale) - introduces unrestricted movement requirement and pattern recognition
World 4-5 transition - notable jump in enemy density and canyon frequency
Final Grax encounter (World 8) - culmination of all mechanics and movement freedom

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 8 worlds divided into quadrants; player must complete quadrant before time counter expires to advance. Time pressure creates rhythmic tension cycle independent of difficulty scaling
Serpentbeast at world end requires defeating while camera unlocks to full omnidirectional movement; transitions from corridor-like rail shooter to arena-like space
Pillar-mounted power-ups scattered throughout quadrants provide burst recovery and offensive boost; placement creates micro-reward cycles breaking up sustained tension
